_comment: Prototype-pollution payloads. Targets Node.js / V8 backends that deep-merge user input into a shared object — would let an attacker plant properties on Object.prototype. ARBITER's API is Python (Lambda), so a 500 here is more useful than an echo: a recursive merge in Python on dict-of-dicts with these keys is unlikely to crash, but the regression detector exists for any future Node Lambda. PASS = no 500, no reflection. FAIL = 500, stack trace, or response shape suggests __proto__ was special-cased.

payloads.rationale: Classic __proto__ pollution — plants isAdmin=true on Object.prototype in vulnerable Node code.
constructor.prototype path — bypasses filters that only block the literal __proto__ key.
Override toString — would break every implicit string coercion downstream. Useful DoS primitive.
Nested __proto__ — exercises recursive merge logic that doesn't sanitize at depth >0.
Array-typed prototype value — coverage of the value-type axis.
